reviews.review_text: I booked superior family room. I request for early check in , which was approved via email. However when I arrived property wasn’t ready. I have to wait another 30-40 minutes. Sofa and dinning chairs were really dirty. Either replace those or give good steam cleaning. Pool has dead mozzies and flies. There were ants in the kitchen. Check the attached pictures

Absolutely loved the pool and enjoyed it with my child. It was well maintained and nicely heated. The rooms are quite dated, although the kitchen has been newly renovated — it’s mainly the rooms that feel a bit old.

This is a nice little park,  a little over priced IMHO.. charged park rates,  and it was the week after school holidays???

Had an ensuite site, the shower was so tiny I could turn around in it.. haha.  The condensation was dripping of the roof because of an ineffective fan.. very dated,  and the shower recess looked so filthy, I had to wear thongs..

And the Possums,  pissed all over our van and car.. for goodness sake get some guards to stop them climbing the trees..

It's a great location,  but we were a little disappointed overall..

The staff were absolutely lovely—warm, welcoming, and genuinely caring. The amenities were spotless and well-maintained, and the park itself was quiet, friendly, and incredibly peaceful. I would highly recommend this place to anyone. A special mention to the staff, whose kind, relaxed, and easygoing nature made the experience even more enjoyable.

Was a fantastic stay, the pool was heated to perfection and very clean and tidy, my kids would have spent our entire stay in the pool if we let them. The staff are friendly and very approachable. The facilities very clean and tidy. I highly recommend staying here if you get the opportunity.

Listed as a big4 park was very disappointing.
Good location but the park itself needs some serious updating. The ensuite site was handy, but very dated and could do with a deep clean and renovation. Laundry facilities were available but old and not well looked after, over priced didn’t wash or dry clothes completely. It’s okay for a short stay, but definitely not what you’d expect for the price. The only positive was the indoor pool, the spa was out of order we were not notified about this as it was something we were looking forward too. Two ladies in reception were very lovely and helpful, unfortunately for the price won’t be returning.

When we first checked in, the room was not clean at all. There was a lot of dirt under the sofas and tables, and it didn’t feel like it had been properly cleaned before we arrived. We also had issues with towels — they were not provided properly, and even after we asked for service, no one came to clean the room.
The place was also very crowded, especially the pool area, which made it hard to enjoy.

Ensuite sites are very cool. Good space, we had a 4 person tent and 4x4 with 270 degree annex and was able to fit everything with ample room. Great site kitchen with bug fridge, freezer, and  multiple appliances. Awesome heated indoor swimming pool.

Kid friendly with playground, games room, and scheduled activities.

Such a great place! We stayed in a cabin over Easter and it was so comfortable. It’s especially good for kids my 2 year old absolutely loved the playground. The hot pool is a massive plus.
What really stood out, though, was the incredibly friendly staff. One of the team members noticed my daughter was a bit scared of the Easter Bunny and kindly gave her a lollipop to cheer her up, which made her day. To top it off, the lovely lady at reception invited her to get her face painted during checkout. I was so touched by these gestures; they made us feel truly welcome and appreciated. Highly recommend for a relaxing family getaway!

We had ensuite site 17 and the grass was so lush. The whole park is :)
Ensuite is a little outdated but still clean and tidy. Also love that they supply a bath mat in there !

Heated pool is such a treat at 29• :)
Pleasantly surprised to find a handwashing station at the dump point 👍🏽 (you don’t see that much)
Reception staff so friendly !
Would stay again for sure :)

I was on an ensuite site.  I have stopped at this park a couple of times in the last few years.  It is very neat and well maintained with an extremely well equipped and clean camp kitchen and general amenities.  There is a very large indoor pool, Rec area and meals room and BBQs.  All staff are pleasant, courteous and helpful.  The only downer is the small shower in the ensuite, but otherwise they are clean and well maintained.  The location is fantastic only requiring a gentle 10 min walk to big brand supermarkets and the CBD.  It's a great place to stay for all camping demographics.  I will happily stay again

The staff were extremely helpful and friendly. Cabin was spotless and modern. Awesome location, within walking distance to everything. First time to Warrnambool and staying there made it a great experience. Could not fault it.
Overdekt zwembad met heel warm water. Heeft wellicht te maken met de warmwater bronnen in de buurt.

3 nights powered caravan site
One of the tidiest caravan parks we have stayed in , Mid week was very quiet with only a few of us oldies around , Friday was busy , Dog friendly, Clean amenities and BBQ area ,
Good easy walk into town and a good solid walk to the beach and flagstaff hill ,
The heated pool is amazing I had a powerd site and was very comfy and plenty of room

I would recommend this place to anyone and everyone. Staff are very welcoming and helpful, the area is very well maintained, bathrooms are kept clean and tidy, very nice pool area, BBQ areas and kitchen facilities are well maintained and very convenient. Honestly would rate them higher than 5 stars if I could.

Absolutely beautiful park. Very friendly staff that welcomed me and my 2 puppies. Spent almost 3 weeks there on holidays and Absolutely loved the place. I left earlier than expected and I now have 4 days credit which I plan to use next month.

We have stayed here twice this time in a unit which was ok but the material  lounge was very dirty covered in marks and stains, the bathroom was very small and in need of a update.
We had previously stayed on a ensuite site and found them to be very old and in need of refurbishment.

decided to stay here and spend a little more for the en-suite, pool and sauna, got there sauna was out of order.. en-suite needs a bit of love and whole place felt a bit run down, very mid experience hence the mid rating.
Studio room feels like a Jail cell. Bed was soft and lumpy. Great location but I think I will upgrade next time .
Nice roomy caravan sites with onsuites

Dieser Park ist eine tolle Alternative zu klassischen Campingplätzen. Er ist sehr modern und luxuriös gestaltet und bietet keine traditionellen Grasplätze. Die Ausstattung ist modern und sauber, und die Preise sind angemessen.

Awful experience. The staff/ owners were terrible. Supposed to be a smoke free park but staff/owners smoked a lot in the car park while scolding our children. Rooms were very dusty, definitely not a clean standard. Spend my first day cleaning the whole lot.

Don’t go there. Staff scolded at our kids for not wearing a helmet however those same people were smoking in the car park while it suppose to be a smoke free park. The cabin was disgusting. My kids spilled some chips and I cleaned the whole lot and the amount of dust and food I pulled from the couch is just disgraceful.

Stayed in inverloch before and was a complete different experience. Wonderful people and very clean.

My family stayed on an ensuite powered site on Australia day weekend 2026, we were very disappointed with a few things. At $219 a night for an ensuite powered site, the shower was tiny and at certain times of the day the ensuite had an earwig infestation, my children were too scared to use the toilet in there because of this.
I noticed the earwings were behind some of the wall panels.
It would be advisable that the caravan park attach draft stoppers to the bottom of the ensuite doors, as they have big gaps at the bottom off the doors which mosquitoes and other insects can get inside, or they need to replace the door on the ensuite and make sure there's no gap at the bottom.
The pool and games room were definitely highlights, my children really enjoyed those.

Location is close walking distance to town, that’s as good as comment I can make. |The on suites were extremely poor!|They have not been refurbished for some time. Photos on their website totally misleading. Our caravan on suite had paint peeling off the lower wall. Dirty long leg spider, along with cobwebs. Look like there was mildew on the ceiling. They really need to look at their cleaning processes because the website photos are not even close to what we had.|Tasman Warrnambool need to review their pricing making sure that visitors have value for money and not price gouge!!|I really hope they review their processes and pricing.

Great location and staff but pricey for state of facilities.
Great shame that the ensuite in on very small site is badly in need of renovation.

Good location close to shops, caravan sites was good but ensuite could use an update!

Pool was not very clean (black ring around  the tiles just above the water level) had to mention 2 times before someone cleaned it.
Had a fantastic time there great staff will be back regards Terry

Camped in a tent from the 26/12 - 28/12 on a ensuite site With my husband 4 children and our golden retriever and have to say we absolutely enjoyed our stay!! Service was amazing, facilities are excellent and clean with plenty to keep our kids occupied. This was our last shot at camping as we have had 3 previous failed attempts 😂 and will say that we will be back. Thank you for restoring our faith in camping fur baby even got a complimentary treat bag and kids got a treasure hunt
Was in 'ensuite' caravan site, sites a bit uneven, ensuite had seen much better days - reception prompt to fix broken lock and apologetic about state of ensuite but won't be using Big 4 again
Clean, spacious rooms. Great facilities with newly renovated pool. There's an infrared sauna but that seems to be out of service.

We spent one night there at an ensuite site. Our bathroom was worn and not very inviting; it desperately needs renovation. The other facilities at the park were quite nice:a great playground, an indoor swimming pool, a well-equipped camp kitchen with a dining area, a lounge, etc. If the bathroom had been more appealing, the park would have deserved 5 stars. A good park for families with children.

We stayed one night on our way down the Great Ocean Road. It was a rainy day and we were thankful for the warm indoor pool  and the dining area inside. Warm welcome ❤️. Just the ensuite needs a little love and more cleaning. Thank you

Great location for shopping and exploring the town . The park was very clean and the amenities are great. Our children use to love coming to figtree when they were younger using the indoor pool . We come when the Jericho cup is on and it’s so convenient catching the bus to the races .

We visited the park in November, 2 couples in a 2 bedroom cabin.
The cabin was amazing, everything you could possibly need was supplied
The size of the cabin is excellent for 4 adults.
Very good location within walking distance to town centre. Highly recommended

Great location and lovely pool, however management was nowhere to be seen when it came to pool supervision, allowing several guests and children to scream, run and dive-bomb with no consequences.
The bed was squeaky and creaked with literally every single movement, even after being replaced. The floor was so dirty white socks went brown within thirty minues of walking around the room, and the table and beside tables were sticky to the point of a book being partially glued down. The internet/tv was so slow to load; it took about 20 minutes to sign into Netflix.
Management was unprofessional, making "jokes" about not being housekeepers and laughing about the dirty floor. This coupled with the seemingly never ending stream of text messages was offputting.
We had a lovely, motel style room. Super clean. Very friendly staff. Walked into town easily. Would recommend.
Was amazing stay good poll and even a suna. The rooms where clean and a nice kitchen bbq on the deck and it was a nice quite spot.
Difficile de juger nous y avons qu une seule nuit ,  les lits etaient confortables et le bungzlow trés bien équipé.

We had a fantastic stay at BIG4 Tasman Holiday Park! It was perfect for our family getaway.

The cabin was clean, well-equipped, and very comfortable, exactly what we needed. The park grounds were tidy and well maintained, and the staff were super friendly and helpful.

Our daughter especially loved the heated pool / playground, and we really appreciated how family-friendly the whole place is. There were plenty of activities to keep the kids entertained.

The location is also ideal, close to the beach, shops, and local attractions. Everything was easy to access, and it felt safe and relaxed throughout our stay.

Highly recommend for families looking for a clean, convenient, and fun holiday park. We’ll definitely be back!
Very clean cabins. Very warm indoor heated pool. Very friendly staff. Good quality people at the venue.
By far the best service we’ve ever received! So many activities for the kids, all staff were so friendly.  Loved getting text message updates about what was happening around the park
Caravan site really wet and boggie.

Stayed in the economy pet friendly cabin, was clean however it did not state on website when booking that the bathroom was separate. Shower was so tiny, I'm only 5'2, 50kg and I struggled for room. Pool was absolutely beautiful but spa and sauna didn't work. Reception was so lovely and accommodating.

We had a cabin overlooking a rubbish tip next door (see photos). The front door didn't close properly and the wall covering was peeling. We complained but were told they couldn't do anything about it. Basically, we paid a premium ("pet friendly") for substandard accommodation

Very Friendly staff
Plenty to do on-site
Swimming pool is heated
Will stay here again
Thank you
Tight  little park well maintained  and friendly staff. Aquick taxi to centre of town
Lovely place to stay, overall cabin was neat and tidy.  Area was quiet and well looked after.  Host were helpful and pleasant
Wish the pool was available and beds could be much better but friendly staff and great location 👍

Thank you to Mel and the team for their exceptional service during our stay. We are staying in the brand new 3 bedroom deluxe cabin, which are the nicest I’ve ever been in (and I’ve stayed at a lot of cabins!)! The layout, amenities and park are great. We will definitely return here.

Outdated in areas, indoor pool needs a revamp.
Nice service tho and close walk to shops

Went to this motel thinking a luxury room was good, I was mistaken, when we arrived we were had seen photos of an excellent basketball but we were wrong it had one ring and was not exciting, afterwards the pool area was full of mold. Rust and because the filters weren't working lots of skin flakes, now the rooms where not modern and up to date at all, when I went to the bathroom it felt like I was living in the 1980s shower took me back in time, lastly the beds were uncomfortable and the couch hadn't been cleaned in quite sometime and was stained although area was good with a short walk to shops.
Warm, welcoming, and helpful staff, great kitchen facilities and a sun-filled indoor pool! Just the best…

Friendly service, great clean park, heated pool and our furry friend even got treats on arrival.
Awesome place to stay for a night or a week, did I mention HEATED POOL.. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Nice place

Booked a ensuite powered site for 1 night. Park is clean and quiet. Reception staff is friendly and they have gas bottle replacement available onsite. The common area and kitchen is clean and well maintained. There are crockery and utensils in the kitchen. Warm and comfortable in the common area during winter. There is also an indoor swimming  pool which we did not utilise. Our enquire toilet is clean. Happy to be here.

Great place to stay with many accommodation choices. The 2 bedroom unit was very nice with plenty of room & beds for a family of 5.
The staff are extremely friendly & helpful and the grounds beautifully kept. Big heated pool & games room was a bonus for the kids
Very nice people and dogs friendly
Old but well kept, pool area is nice, small park. Friendly staff
Stopping only one night might mean any reviews are a bit qualified. Really impressed with reception. Two sites for our vans were OK at best. Facilities were spotless.
Great caravan Park with swings heated indoor pool grass and concrete sites, ensuites and camping.  Well maintained and short walk to shops and the pub. Highly recommended.

We had an amazing stay at BIG4 Tasman Holiday Park in Warrnambool! The staff were incredibly warm and welcoming, making us feel right at home from the moment we arrived. The park was peaceful and not too crowded, which made it the perfect spot for a relaxing weekend or a longer holiday getaway. Highly recommended for families or anyone looking to unwind and enjoy a beautiful, well-kept location.

Great park. Close to town.
Tidy clean and lovely managers

Called 2 days before to make booking as advertised price suited . No answer or return of call . Add says plenty space available so I turn up and the lady says more than double price . I said no wanted advertised price . I was told bad luck is out of my control . And they attitude was really bad for customer service . Left there went to port fairy caravan park only 20 min away got good service and great place to stay

The low score is mainly because of the manager Mel. We find her dishonest and unprofessional.

We booked Deluxe Cabin 89 for 2-5 Feb. As soon as we checked in, we noticed a disgusting smell in the room and found a roasted chicken (photos available) in the microwave. After contacting Mel, she said that she cleaned everywhere except the microwave after the last guest checked out. She removed the chicken and cleaned the microwave. But the room is still filled with the smell of rotten chicken. It's there for at least a day, and it was over 30 degrees, imagine the smell!

Mel proposed Cabin 63 (according to Mel is the only one available), and promised that she would refund us with the difference of the two suites as well as waiving the fee of the first night. So, we moved to Cabin 63, which is not a deluxe cabin so costs lower than the one we booked.

In the next morning, she refused to waive the one-night fee and only would refund the room difference. Her reason was we refused to stay in Cabin 89, but it was her proposal to move and promise to waive the night’s fee. When asked about her promises, she said she never said anything like that!

When we checked out on 5th Feb, Mel said that there was “something” wrong with the system therefore it would take a few hours before she could refund the room difference.

As of today, no refund was made to us. We called them a few times and they either did not answer the phone or ignored our request.

So, we had a rotten chicken in the room, had to pack and move during the evening to a cabin with downgrade, being lied to multiple times, and paid the full price for a deluxe cabin! We won’t come back because we don’t want to deal with Mel and her bag of lies. If you’re going there, I suggest you record everything she says.

Lovely central location. Walking distance into shopping mall. Grass powered sites are all very tight and require precision reverse parking. Most sites have en-suite, I wish we had known prior to booking as the sites without en-suite have to use only main bathrooms that have 2 showers and 2 toilets. Bathroom is very outdated and has shower curtains, minimal space for changing. No family bathroom facilities. Indoor pool has been tastefully updated, so too has playground and mixed sport court. Staff are very friendly.

The staff were extremely friendly, these are the things l liked
- they made sure all kids received an Easter egg even if the kids didn't find one on the Easter egg hunt.
- They had a Easter bunny hunt for the adult which was a welcome change.
- the pool was a perfect temperature to be able to chill in it for a length period
-  park was very well maintained
- our cabin had 1960 bathroom but not one tile was broken and it was extremely clean, l was very impressed.
- new modern kitchen with no chipped crockery :)
- Regular text about events and when commencing
- free games in games and family room
- walking distance to town
- free shuttle bus to/fromm to rsl
- you can tell the park is taken care of.

Improvements can be made
- better signage about how to use new Owen
- internet is poor but that's a Warrnambool issue not park issue but my understanding plans are in place for upgrade.
- would like a jumping pillow as most Big 4 have this
- the onsite cafe on site 1, l think is independent, is expensive and poor quality, l ordered the fish & chips on Easter Friday for 1 person @$20.  Fish was dry, very small handful of soggy chips, not worth the money.

We would definitely stay their again.

what a fantastic fantastic place. Warner is fantastic facilities at this park and easy access to the city and to the beach. Highly recommended.||we stayed in our 7 RV with ease witha Private ensuite. Park was clean and tidy with residences and other visitors staying friendly

Park manager Mel was fantastic, went above and beyond for the first and with a smile on her fave. Always something going on for the kids during our Easter holiday stay.
We had a great time and would visit again
#44 Ensuite site

First time @ Tasman Big 4 Holiday Park , a much needed break .

Location is the draw card for this property!

Arrived @ 830pm on our first night, booked for 3 , reception closed our keys in envelope in guest box.

Unit was choice of accommodation , cleanliness  very poor , couch all stained , all beds not made on arrival also fans in bathroom also dining area were absolutely filthy.

Minimal towel’s during the duration of our stay , only was  provided 4 and one face washer for  our 3 night and 4 day stay .

Addressed this issue with reception, to be told by manager that it’s not a hotel and it’s  general families that stay in the units, I informed manager we are a family , and 4 towels also one face washer isn’t acceptable for a family for that many days.

On our second night, on return from being out all day to a unit infested by ants, in bathroom , kitchen also all down hallway and in one room , reception once again unattended, after hrs number not easily accessible.

To contact reception the following day , after a  4 hr drive home as accommodation unable to be used , to be advised we should  have called reception to assist us  , also to be informed that the cleaner stated there wasn’t no ants.

Wouldn’t recommend this property , definitely not value for money.
Met veel plezier hier gestaan

Some of the en suites are a bit tired but spotlessly clean. The indoor pool and other facilities are terrific.
Managers Scott and Mel are fantastic.
Disappointed with unit as quite old and bathroom needs updating
We had a powered site, park was great and  close to shops, Scott the manager was fantastic , will definitely be back.
It was only a basic cabin we had just 1 night it did the job. Close to the shops

The park was really nice and amenities were clean and in a good location, it even had a heated pool and sauna. Its a really good location close to the city centre. We have stayed at alot of caravan park as we are travelling Aus, the managers specifically at this park were fantastic. They were lovely and really helpful. We loved staying here, thanks so much.

Scott was fantastic! He helped us with an electrical issue on the caravan, which turned out to be a faulty cable on our end. He was always friendly and even called a local electrician for us. We couldn’t have asked for better service!

The facilities are a bit older but were always clean and in good working order. The indoor heated pool was in great condition, and the kids loved it.

The location is excellent—everything is just a five-minute walk away.

There was even a food truck on-site, serving coffee in the morning and food and dessert at night.

We’ll definitely be back. Thanks again for all your help, Scott!
Sehr schön angelegt und sehr sauberer Platz
The best got to get a late check out it was good not to leave as early would definitely stay again
Great facilities and services. Friendly, helpful staff. Very clean.
Great location, good facilities
Very welcoming and staff great but unfortunately all a bit tired

We had a deluxe cabin which was very well presented and clean. The site carpark was a good size as well we had a medium SUV and there was plenty of room, we were able to have all the doors fully open while we were unloading/loading. The park itself has great facilities and a fantastic location.

What was once a fantastic park, has been taken over by a greedy corporate company.  Since taking over the caravan park the caravan site prices have increased from $90.00 per night to $200 per night in the last 3 years. We have decided to book a 3 bedroom air B&B for 3 couples next year for a price of $150 a night per couple, $50 cheaper than a piece of dirt at this caravan park. No brainer really.

The kids loved the pool, tennis court, playground and games areas. Our kitchenette had basic utensils and servingware, which was helpful for breakfasts. Service was great, and all the staff that we encountered were very friendly.

Can't get a site for 3 nights . Because I'm a local .. last time I stayed at a Tasman , was Geelong , back in 2018..  That was a bad experience, so I guess I'm not too fussed about them not letting me and my van to check in here .
But I won't use a Tasman park in the future. I don't think it's professional, not letting certain people in because of their address

We had a fantastic time. Stayed for 2 nights in unit. Rooms were lovely and clean. Staff were very friendly helped us with pool towels, scavenger hunt, tennis courts. Pool was great kids loved it perfect temperature. Highly recommend great location too very central to everything.
Room was fairly dirty, no sofa as advertised, garden furniture quite worn and “en suite” toilet actually outside. Well-located.

After over 15 years of staying at this park every summer we can no longer justify coming back and paying the overpriced rates. Since Tasman’s has taken over, it’s has increased by double, making it the most expensive caravan park in the area. If the quality has improved we would be happy to accept such huge increases but unfortunately the long list of defects seems to be growing each year.
Tasman’s will be losing a lot of summer regulars and sadly they don’t seem to care. Shame as it has the potential. Don’t recommend for the price, you can find better more reasonably priced in the area.

My wifes family have been coming here for the last 36 years. I have been going for the past 20 odd years my children have loved this park and learnt to ride their bikes and swim here but since Tasman have taken over the price has more than doubled in two years with no real upgrades to facilities apart from the pool. The caravan ensuites are falling apart ect. The park has been flooded out twice and Tasman could care less that cars and caravans were damaged. We have now been informed that next year the price is jumping up substantial again. This last price rise has forced us to make the hard decision to stop the family tradition of attending this caravan park. We are a total of eight sites and once again Tasman could care less. Go to any other park and they will be cheaper and you will be looked after better and have nicer facilities.

Huge price increase for camping.
Having holidayed in this park at Christmas in our caravan for over 15 years, we have been forced to look elsewere as a result of the huge price increase. Ensuite sites are going up by $60 per night bringing the fee up to $198 per night. This is an extra $840  for our usual 2 weeks. With poorly maintained sites and old facilities we find this very unreasonable and will not be returning. Other parks offer better facilities for much lower fees.
Reiner.

We have stayed here for the last time. Sadly this park now has ownership that has champagne tastes with prices being so high over Christmas that more than 15 families will no longer give anymore money towards this caravan park. Not a place that the fees make sense considering what they provide.

The park is in a good location to the city. It has a nice pool. Basic amenities. If travelling with caravan be warned.. this park is TIGHT. We were put on a site that was too small and offered no onsite parking for our vehicle. This was highly annoying and inconvenient when staying for 6 days. To be told by the office " oh well, just go park out the front" was not an acceptable solution and not an occurrence we've ever encountered, having travelled extensively throughout caravan parks all over australia. Fortunately the manager is a lovely and helpful man who went out of his way make arrangements for our vehicle to parked near our van, which was much appreciated given we were travelling with a toddler, so we were very appreciative of that.

Well maintained park. Very compact and close to town
Well equipped kitchen, heated pool. Playground for small kids

Booked a cabin for 5 people for 4 nights. Very old cabin for $1400. Bunk beds and single beds were very unsteady and made sounds on every movement. The 3 people sleeping in the room with the bunk beds had to move their mattresses on the floor and sleep on the floor. We left after staying for 1 night.
When we first arrived, we opened we fridge and a month flew out of it and flew into my father’s mouth.
Would not recommend, will not be staying there again.
Had a great stay, clean tidy park

Friendly and professional staffs, changed two types of rooms during our stay, both are neat and clean, even the cleaner we met in the morning are very kind, love the indoor pool, kids enjoyed the swims over there especially when the weather was not hot and you can’t swim outdoors. Just a bit pity that the sauna room was not in use and couldn’t try that.
The thing was not very good is when we lived in unit 9, everytime we drove out, someone will take our parking space regardless there are clear number on the ground, and one night we need to park at the visitors parking space.
The entrance of the holiday park doesn’t have a secure gate was another concern, basically any car/people can come in, if can be upgraded later on that will be great.
After all, it’s a nice experience to stay here.

The ensuite caravan sites are very small and uneven.  We  were searching for a site in the Warrnambool area for our caravan and looked at a number of parks.  The Tasman park in Lava Street had heaps of ensuite sites available at a weekend when ensuite sites are generally hard to find.  Not at all surprising they had so many vacancies because of the extremely poor quality of their ensuite sites.  Further, they were quite expensive.

We (a group of three parties) stayed at the power camp site during the Cup Day weekend 2024. My friend's fishing gears and backpad located behind the camp tents were disappeared. We think those gears were stolen. this camp site has no lock gate at the front door and everyone can access easily. Big 4 needs to think about how to resolve this issue. Thanks

We stayed here for 2 nights and it was very nice compared to other places. Unfortunately when we were visiting the pool was under going renovations but we didnt need it and it looked really large. The rooms are functional with kitchen utensils and everything you need. The beds are comfy. the games room is very nice with ping pong and arcade.
Highly recommended you to stay at the park , Friendly people clean rooms ,we would stay again , 😁

Excellent Park. Stayed one night. Cabin was great with our little dog. Very clean and quite. Great over the phone service and easy booking as we arrivedafter closing time. So close to everything. Great camping sites as well and plenty of shade. Overall fantastic. Ross

Great cabin for a group or families but there is not much insulation so you can hear people in the cabins next to you if they are heavy on their feet or kids running around.  Parking is available at each cabin but can get a bit tight if the park is busy!  Close to main road and town but also quiet from road noise which is good.  Didn’t use the park facilities but if you have kids there is heaps to do.  Check in and out process quite easy

Un magnifique camping situé en plein dans la ville dans une zone pavillonnaire. Il dispose d'une piscine intérieure, de barbecue, d'une salle d'arcades, d'une laverie...
Si vous arrivez après les horaires d'ouverture de l'accueil, il faudra appeler le numéro affiché sur la porte de cette dernière pour avoir le code du coffre contenant vos informations

Fabulous. We were able to take our fur family, brilliantly appointed cabin, lovely people.
Will definitely be back soon.
Friendly staff, the pool was great

Nice campsite in a useful location to town. Facilities are good including a big indoor pool. Only negatives was the bathrooms could maybe do with an upgrade and the kitchen didn’t have any utensils. Would probably stay here again if passing through.

Poor WiFi connection especially cabin 90. On complaining to staff they didn’t help much apart from blaming it on Warrnambool and saying “it’s like this all over Warrnambool”
Then got to know the router is kept only in reception so only the cabins closer to reception will get good WiFi signals.

My mum and I are from Melbourne and went for an overnight trip to Warrnambool to try and spot some whales. We stayed here last night and I have absolutely nothing negative to say about our stay. We were allocated to a deluxe cabin and were delighted to find how clean, modern and comfortable it was. The lady that greeted us at reception was very friendly and welcoming. Accomodation is within walking distance to the main town however due to the weather, we had to drive in.
I would 100% stay here again and will be recommending it to everyone.

Very old and small sites for vans.
Concrete slabs are broken , ensuites are old as the hills and the boundary fence is old and falling apart and ready to fall down on you. Only plus is that it’s close to shopping precinct
Looking for a place to stay and stumbled across it while turning around. Staff were really friendly and helpful. Would stay here again.
Good
Excellent place to stay for family and friends
Loved this park. We had an ensuite site very clean facilities. The children had a blast in the pool which was also very clean.

You Know what this is nice, A little nice...
Someday We were going into our room and guess what? A dent in the floor and the pool is soo nice, but the playground is a little wet rn, I suggest That the dent was in Cabin 53,
Good luck.
Note The Refrigerator Smells Wired But Just some stuff.
Central with short stroll to CBD and restaurants

Very disappointed in this Park, we arrived off the Ferry at 6.30 am on a Sunday Morning  very cold and tired.|The park sent a text message asking what time we would be checking in, I replied 10.am, knowing this was early, but being winter knew the park would not be busy and told them we had just got off the ferry.|The reply I received back was check.is not to 12.pm.|So we park up a Bunnings car park in the cold to wait.|When we arrived the reception was 0 personality, as well as there being a caravan there which was also on the boat, all set up, so I can only assume that they checked in before 12. Also asked for a site so we didn’t have to unhook, and was given a site that we had to u hook. This Caravan Park doesn’t meet big 4 standard as well as when the Caravan Park is pretty empty, can’t see why we were refused access early as the grounds were not being attended too as well.|Maybe they have rules, but in winter is it necessary  to adhere to them and not just give your Big 4 Members so Nice Hospitality |Will not be back and would not reckoned

What a wonderful lady at reception on the 5th May We were staying in a tent with an unsuited and became ill overnight My husband went up to the office as soon as they opened and came back 5 minutes later with a key for a cabin for the next 3 days of our stay and we could move straight in Never has a unit been more welcome Would absolutely recommend this great park and the wonderful people who run it May not be the most beautiful or modern accommodation but we will forever be grateful for their kindness Thank you from the bottom of our hearts
wifi did not work but other than that, place was in a convenient location with comfortable beds

We had a great stay at the park on our way across to Perth. The staff were very friendly and hospitable. We had a three night stay in the pet friendly cabin which was perfect for our needs. Walking distance to the main streets and all shops/amenities. Kids loved the playground and pool (although it would have been even better if it was slightly warmer!).
All in all a great stay, thanks guys!

Nice rooms and friendly hosts.
TVreception not great but only minor issue.
Highly recommend.

‘Indoor heated pool’  is ice cold, I’m pregnant and was too scared to enter the pool as it was so cold, my nephew came out with purple lips after 10 minutes.
Manager and state OPS manager are aware that their heat pump is broken (has been for months) but obviously not willing to invest the money into fixing this.

Other than this, our room was neat, spacious, comfortable and had good amenities.
Room layout is not ergonomically being that you have to look sideways to watch tv.
Wouldn’t stay again due to the lack of care from management.

We had booked this accommodation over winter for the indoor pool facilities. It states on the website and out the front the pool is heated. However the pool is very cold. Every single person in the pool said how cold it was. Basically the same temp as a regular outside pool. The pump is clearly broken, they are well aware of it (other reviews from months ago mentioned this) and not wanting to fix it. I took this to management who was very rude about it. There solution to the heat pump cutting out and not holding the temp was to try to get stuff to turn it up more often.
Also the wifi dosnt work at all and the cabin bedrooms weren’t big enough for a porter cot. We checked out 2 nights early.

The toilet needs attaching to the floor, the floor in the bathroom is dangerously sloped and the shower section of the bath is impossible to stand and use, the extraction fans need a good dusting and they need to make sure people actually clean the things they use, the cups were grubby!!

Had to prove on the second day that I was still supposed to be there, apparently there was a problem with their computer system - good thing I kept the paper work
Good location and with dog friendly cabins is always a plus :)
Possibly the BEST cabin we have ever stayed in. Spacious, solid, roomy, well equipped. If only all tourist park accommodation was this nice!

We had a fantastic stay at Warrnambool Tasman Holiday Parks in the deluxe 3 bedroom cabin and it sure was deluxe!

The cabin was particularly spacious, very clean and well stocked with everything you would need.

A well equipped park with great, clean facilities, we especially loved the indoor pool!

All of the staff were really friendly and helpful.

I would stay again and highly recommend staying here for a affordable family holiday or group of friends!

Friendly, cabins pretty good everything's there that you needed bit pricey but I definitely stay again it's a lovely park for family children playing dog and pet friendly nice and tidy clean facilities definitely a five-star more like 10 🥰

Super friendly service
Still the nicest people 😄
Loved it nice quiet park with lots of things to do.

Fantastic place to spend some great family time.
My daughter and 5 grandchildren from 9,10,11,12 and 15 loved every second of our stay

The park was lovely and clean, the staff were nice to deal with. The mattress in our cabin was hard as a rock which meant we barely slept and the pool was very cold and not well heated. This meant you could only swim for a short time. The kids pool was even colder than the large one.
Internet either didn’t work or was terrible and unusable.
The camp kitchen said it closed at 10 but was locked at 8.

Great place to stay with plenty of ammenities.

Too much that we were not able to fully use it. Would be a great place to bring a family for a vacation.

There is a function room as well as a playground and pool .

One thing that I had really enjoyed about this place was that there was an allocated parking lot right in front our our unit

The rooms that we had was well sized and well equipped but to be honest a little bit dated.
Can tell that it was painted over wallpaper and the shower room was really too tiny.

On the last night as well there was a large spider lurking over our bed.

We stayed here on a powered site in our caravan, the facilities were close by and each site had its own ensuite,  if you choose to use it. The indoor pool was fantastic! It was a wonderful place to stay and we would definitely come back!!

The pool was closed when we were there. The facilities are clean and adequate. It's in our opinion over priced, the park has no views except other caravans and just felt hemmed in. Staff friendly. Ok for a stop over.

I booked the deluxe cabin and it was nice and spacious.
Unfortunately the Queen bed was rock hard and uncomfortable.
I suggest you upgrade your mattress in cabin 89.
Also the pool was closed for the whole week which happens I can understand that but the management should of told me that the pool wasn't going to be open when I called up and made my booking. I also suggest that you offer a small discount or anything else for the disappointment caused to the guests by the pool closure.
I saw a family with kids all in their bathers ready to go for a swim and when they saw the pool closed the kids were devastated.
This is just unfair when you pay for the premium /deluxe option.
Apart from the above, the park is clean and in good proximity to the city centre.

The kids pool had a spider in it, enough said.
Very bad experience from someone coming here for over 10 years, the previous operators were much better!
Great place, close to everything you need. Nice little getaway
Lots to suit all size vans and set ups.  Pool was a little cold but a great size and it is indoors. Playground is great for the children and close to Warrnambool city centre.
We really enjoyed our stay here. Beautiful areas to walk along.

Room was lovely but missing many amenities which should have been here for the price we pay. Had to go to office to fix too many times.  Way too expensive and not enough activities for the kids seeing as it’s a big 4. The lady at the front was very apologetic and accommodating but shouldn’t happen in the first place.

It was very central and the room was clean. The tv required re tuning and there were no instructions. My husband managed. A/C didn't work well. The filters were badly clogged. It was really cold so little warmth.

We had a great stay here in a one bedroom apartment. It was clean and the beds comfortable. The kitchen was well equipped for all meal cooking. The games room was super fun. The park grounds were very well maintained. We will be back for other stays

Brilliant accommodation. Staying here for 12 days. Best accommodation ever.
Definitely recommend it. Family room was brilliant. Congratulations, Tasman 5 starts at least. We stayed here 4 yrs ago too, every bit as good as last time we were here.
Great indoor pool, 5 min walk to main street, new cabins and community bonfire was great.
Thank you for our delightful stay! The cabin was lovely, we were well looked after and the customer service was fantastic.

The cabin is awesome but the Wi-Fi is absolutely terrible. The only thing you can watch on the tvs are live because when you try and go onto YouTube and Netflix it needs to load every 5 seconds. And I'm not exaggerating when I say every 5 seconds. You press play, watch 5 seconds, then wait for 3 minutes for it to load, repeat. Anyway, though the wifi is terrible, the facilities are pretty good. As I said before, the rooms in the cabin are amazing. Plus the staff were really kind and helpful. 4 stars.

It was a short one-night stay for us, but we had a lovely experience. We arrived late after driving the entire day along the Great Ocean Road, and even though the receptionist had already closed, they provided a quick and easy check-in process.

We especially appreciated the bed warmer during winter. We didn't have the opportunity to try out many of the facilities offered here, and unfortunately, the pool was undergoing maintenance during our stay. However, overall, it was a quick and pleasant experience!
In heart of Warranbool. Close  to the shops, the beach is not far away. The swimming pool would be good during the summer as it's renovated.
Staff were very cold and unfriendly almost as if they didn’t want us to stay. TV didn’t work at all.
Good all-round holiday park. Well situated in Warrnambool, the staff are lovely and helpful. Stayed here three times now in both cabins and a motel-style room. Happy to return for more.
Excellent park. Extremely obliging staff. Great sites. Immaculately looked after and clean.

Awesome stay at Figtree Tasman Holiday Parks (Warrnambool). Bedrooms and toilets were clean. Located near the Great Ocean Road and good choice of accommodation if doing a road trip. Would’ve been a better experience if the WiFi was more reliable and efficient though. Overall still a pleasant stay.
